quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g., 'North outsold West by $761.53 — a 54% gap' and per-product revenue dollars rather than bare rankings.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data — e.g., 'Reprice or grow Sprocket volume; at $4.75 per unit it trails every other product' and 'Investigate West's shortfall ($1,414.82)... mirror North's product mix.'

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like 'Gadget's high unit price drives its top rank despite modest units sold' and the 54% best-to-weakest regional gap rather than restating totals.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g., "North outsold West by $761.53 — a 54% gap" and all product/region revenues are given in dollars.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data, e.g. "Reprice or grow Sprocket volume; at $4.75 per unit it trails every other product" and "mirror North's product mix to narrow the 54% regional revenue gap."

insight_density.why: Multiple actionable, non-obvious observations appear, such as "Gadget's high unit price drives its top rank despite modest units sold" and Sprocket's low $4.75 unit price explaining its revenue lag.

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. 'North outsold West by $761.53 — a 54% gap' and per-product revenue totals plus a $204.94 median.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names concrete products/regions and figures, e.g. 'Reprice or grow Sprocket volume; at $4.75 per unit it trails every other product.'

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations like Gadget's 'high unit price drives its top rank despite modest units sold' and the 54% best-vs-weakest regional gap rather than just restating totals.
